task_description: I2: Extract Component CSS from Templates — Move component CSS from inline <style> blocks in templates to static CSS files (one per component). Component templates become pure HTML structure. The {% theme_head %} tag includes all component CSS once. Separates 'what it looks like' (CSS) from 'what it is' (HTML structure).

checklist.action: checkout fresh branch task/I2-extract-component-css from main
current structure — read all component templates in templates/djust_theming/components/, identify every <style> block
read theme_tags.py theme_head tag to understand how CSS is currently included
read static/djust_theming/ to understand existing CSS file structure
proposed refactoring approach (step by step) — how to extract each component's CSS to a static file
create static/djust_theming/css/components.css with consolidated component CSS
remove <style> blocks from 6 component templates, keep only HTML structure
update theme_head.html to include component CSS via {% static %} link
preserve ALL existing behavior — rendered output should look identical
write list of all changed files to .pipeline-state/task-I2-extract-component-css-changes.txt
run full test suite (read-only, no source modifications)
cross-ref analysis plan vs refactoring — flag unimplemented changes
verify behavior preserved — CSS output should be identical
read .pipeline-state/task-I2-extract-component-css-analysis.md to understand what was refactored
add/update docstrings for modified public APIs
update existing docs that reference moved/renamed code
verify no broken internal links or orphaned docs
read .pipeline-state/task-I2-extract-component-css-changes.txt for list of files modified
git remote -v — verify github remote exists
git add -u to stage ALL modified tracked files
git add each file from changes.txt to catch new files

9.name: Code Review
9.subagent_prompt: You are reviewing PR #{pr_number} for a refactor. Project: {project_path}. PR targets {pr_target_branch}. Step 0: Read .pipeline-state/*-analysis.md for the original analysis. Step 1: Run git diff {pr_target_branch}...HEAD, read changed files. Step 2: Cross-ref analysis vs implementation — verify all planned changes were made. CRITICAL: Verify behavior is preserved — refactor must NOT change functionality. Step 3: Review for correctness, security, testing, quality, docs, perf. Step 4: Run project test suite, report pass/fail. Step 5: Decide APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT. Step 6 MANDATORY: Post review to GitHub with gh pr review {pr_number} --comment --body '<formatted review>'. If REQUEST_CHANGES use --request-changes. Step 7: Output verdict.

checklist.action: review: correctness, security, testing, quality, docs, perf
run full test suite, report pass/fail
post review to GitHub: gh pr review --comment
if REQUEST_CHANGES: fix issues, re-commit, re-push, re-run stage 9

11.name: Merge PR
11.subagent_prompt: Merge PR #{pr_number} in {project_path}. Run: gh pr merge {pr_number} --squash --delete-branch. Do NOT run gh pr review --approve. Output PR_MERGED or MERGE_FAILED.

12.name: Retrospective
12.subagent_prompt: Write retrospective for refactor: {task_description}. Project: {project_path}. PR #{pr_number}. Rate quality 1-5, what went well, lessons learned. MANDATORY: Post retro as gh pr comment {pr_number} --body '<formatted retro>'. MANDATORY: Append to .pipeline-log.md. Output RETRO_COMPLETE.
